name: Max Steiner
description: Austrian-born American music composer and conductor (1888–1971)
abstract: Maximilian Raoul Steiner (10 May 1888 – 28 December 1971) was an Austrian composer and conductor who emigrated to America and became one of Hollywood's greatest musical composers. Steiner was a child prodigy who conducted his first operetta when he was twelve and became a full-time professional, proficient at composing, arranging, and co…

about_quotes.speaker: Howie Shear
about_quotes.evidence: Max Steiner from the early days of film was one of the greats.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howie Shear interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: I find it hard to believe that it was Max Steiner. Why Max Steiner would have wanted to involve himself with the second half of a jazz band chart I could never begin to understand why.

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I got to work with Johnny Green and Max Steiner and Alfred Newman, Lionel Newman, and the guys
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I saw a friend... we started talking about Max Steiner and Ray Heindorf and all of these people that I lived with every day
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998
